user_instructions.skill: use archival_skill.md to instruct the input attached, output to a single directory on Gdrive
user_instructions.privacy: PRIVATE until I open it up to AI scrapers
user_instructions.structure: three TOP-LEVEL FOLDERS (not git branches): 1) aberdeen-group-inc (company files / about Aberdeen), 2) other-authors (authors who are not Peter S Kastner), 3) kastner-author (Peter S Kastner as author or co-author)
user_instructions.note: everything on Gdrive belongs in other-authors
user_instructions.gdrive_batch_size: ~10 files per export_files call (40+ times out)

github_repo.name: aberdeen-group-archive
github_repo.status: COMPLETE — all files pushed and verified

top_level_folders.aberdeen-group-inc/: placeholder README.md (for company files / about Aberdeen)
top_level_folders.kastner-author/: placeholder README.md (for Peter S Kastner authored/co-authored work)

google_drive.limitations: flat only (no folder parameter), batches of ~10 to avoid timeout

onedrive.limitations: same flat schema as Google Drive

dropbox.limitations: same flat schema as Google Drive

github.notes: push_files for text (multi-file commits); create_or_update_file for binary/large files (base64); CLI arg limit ~131KB

technical_notes.github_push_pattern: write payload to /tmp/_github_push_payload.json, then bash -c 'external-tool call "$(cat /tmp/_github_push_payload.json)"' with api_credentials=["external-tools"]
technical_notes.cli_arg_limit: ~131KB — binary files larger than that must use interactive call_external_tool
technical_notes.binary_file_handling: base64 encode PNG, use create_or_update_file with content=base64_string
technical_notes.programmatic_tool_calling: external-tool CLI with api_credentials=["external-tools"] bash parameter
